citations.description: test_composite_rejects_wrong_resource_token — unit test only (asyncio.run, no ASGI transport)
test_missing_required_scope_403_and_present_200 — ASGI integration test covers 200/403/401 but NOT the wrong-resource stub-verifier case

findings.criterion: Integration test asserts: valid static token → 200; unknown/invalid token → 401; a principal lacking a required_scope → 403; and a token whose resource ≠ auth_resource_server_url (injected via a stub verifier in the composite) → 401 at the composite regardless of the sub-verifier (negative controls included).
findings.detail: The 200/401/403 sub-assertions are covered by the ASGI integration test `test_missing_required_scope_403_and_present_200` (and `test_http_auth_valid_token_200_and_missing_token_401`). However, the 'token whose resource ≠ auth_resource_server_url injected via a stub verifier in the composite → 401' sub-assertion is only covered by a UNIT test (`test_composite_rejects_wrong_resource_token`, which uses asyncio.run against the CompositeTokenVerifier directly), NOT by an ASGI-level integration test as the criterion explicitly requires. No ASGI integration test exists that injects a stub verifier returning a wrong-resource AccessToken and asserts a 401 HTTP response.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
